data.settleTest: Only the unfolding of the next 12 months settles this: whether large operators actually issue open, multi-vendor rack-PDU tenders during current framework terms, or keep spend locked. No purchasable dataset today states what fraction of the 40% is contract-foreclosed for rack-PDU specifically — the cited deals cover UPS/switchgear/skids, not rack PDUs, and even those preserve 'multi-vendor' flexibility. The claim hinges on future tender behavior, a foreclosure gate that may or may not bind.

researchQueries.query: Do Digital Realty, Equinix, or major hyperscalers' 2025-2026 framework/SCA agreements (Schneider $373M, HD Hyundai $1.1B) explicitly cover rack-level PDUs, or only UPS/switchgear/skids — and do they name single-source or multi-vendor terms?

Are there open, multi-vendor rack-PDU tenders issued by large operators (e.g., EU 2026 data-center rack-PDU RFPs) during active framework terms, indicating rack PDUs remain competitively bid outside long-term deals?
